author: s1na
content:
I've been trying to understand the reasoning behind the 4-5 months block retention by CLs, and found the answer here: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/v1.7.0-alpha.5/specs/phase0/p2p-interface.md#why-are-blocksbyrange-requests-only-required-to-be-served-for-the-latest-compute_min_epochs_for_block_requests-epochs
It arrives at 33024 epochs of retention by picking the maximal safety decay constant (i.e 100%) for WSP calculation. For mainnet we are on safety decay of 10%, hence WSP is 3533 and *not* 33024 epochs.

So far I am convinced it is a conservative value picked because the difference in storage size doesn't make a big difference. It is not out of the requirement of being able to replay blocks which are 4 months old.
